016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.microservice.resources;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.StateEnum.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
023
024import java.text.*;
025import java.util.*;
026import java.util.concurrent.*;
027import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
028import java.util.logging.*;
029import java.util.logging.Formatter;
030import java.util.regex.*;
031
032/**
033 * Log entry formatter.
034 *
035 * <p>
036 * Uses three simple parameter for configuring log entry formats:
037 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
038 *    <li>
039 *       <c>dateFormat</c> - A {@link SimpleDateFormat} string describing the format for dates.
040 *    <li>
041 *       <c>format</c> - A string with <c>{...}</c> replacement variables representing predefined fields.
042 *    <li>
043 *       <c>useStackTraceHashes</c> - A setting that causes duplicate stack traces to be replaced with 8-character
044 *        hash strings.
045 * </ul>
046 *
047 * <p>
048 * This class converts the format strings into a regular expression that can be used to parse the resulting log file.
049 */
050@SuppressWarnings({
051   "java:S2143" // Public API built on SimpleDateFormat/Date for Formatter compatibility; getDateFormat() returns DateFormat as a contract.
052})
053public class LogEntryFormatter extends Formatter {
054
055   private static String hashCode(Throwable t) {
056      int i = 0;
057      while (nn(t)) {
058         for (var e : t.getStackTrace())
059            i ^= e.hashCode();
060         t = t.getCause();
061      }
062      return Integer.toHexString(i);
063   }
064
065   private ConcurrentHashMap<String,AtomicInteger> hashes;
066   private DateFormat df;
067   private String format;
068   private Pattern rePattern;
069
070   private Map<String,Integer> fieldIndexes;
071
072   /**
073    * Create a new formatter.
074    *
075    * @param format
076    *    The log entry format.  e.g. <js>"[{date} {level}] {msg}%n"</js>
077    *    The string can contain any of the following variables:
078    *    <ol>
079    *       <li><js>"{date}"</js> - The date, formatted per <js>"Logging/dateFormat"</js>.
080    *       <li><js>"{class}"</js> - The class name.
081    *       <li><js>"{method}"</js> - The method name.
082    *       <li><js>"{logger}"</js> - The logger name.
083    *       <li><js>"{level}"</js> - The log level name.
084    *       <li><js>"{msg}"</js> - The log message.
085    *       <li><js>"{threadid}"</js> - The thread ID.
086    *       <li><js>"{exception}"</js> - The localized exception message.
087    *       <li><js>"{traceId}"</js> - The active OpenTelemetry trace ID (empty when OTel is absent or no span is active).
088    *       <li><js>"{spanId}"</js> - The active OpenTelemetry span ID (empty when OTel is absent or no span is active).
089    *    </ol>
090    * @param dateFormat
091    *    The {@link SimpleDateFormat} format to use for dates.  e.g. <js>"yyyy.MM.dd hh:mm:ss"</js>.
092    * @param useStackTraceHashes
093    *    If <jk>true</jk>, only print unique stack traces once and then refer to them by a simple 8 character hash
094    *    identifier.
095    */
096   @SuppressWarnings({
097      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for console command logic
098   })
099   public LogEntryFormatter(String format, String dateFormat, boolean useStackTraceHashes) {
100      this.df = new SimpleDateFormat(dateFormat);
101      if (useStackTraceHashes)
102         hashes = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
103
104      fieldIndexes = new HashMap<>();
105
106      // @formatter:off
107      format = format
108         .replace("{date}", "%1$s")
109         .replace("{class}", "%2$s")
110         .replace("{method}", "%3$s")
111         .replace("{logger}", "%4$s")
112         .replace("{level}", "%5$s")
113         .replace("{msg}", "%6$s")
114         .replace("{threadid}", "%7$s")
115         .replace("{exception}", "%8$s")
116         .replace("{traceId}", "%9$s")
117         .replace("{spanId}", "%10$s");
118      // @formatter:on
119
120      this.format = format;
121
122      // Construct a regular expression to match this log entry.
123      int index = 1;
124      var re = new StringBuilder();
125
126      // The parser state machine uses four states while scanning for format placeholders.
127      // In state S1 we are looking for a percent sign.
128      // In state S2 we have found a percent sign and are looking for a number.
129      // In state S3 we have found a number and are looking for a dollar sign.
130      // In state S4 we have found a dollar sign and are looking for an 's'.
131      var state = S1;
132      int i1 = 0;
133      for (var i = 0; i < format.length(); i++) {
134         var c = format.charAt(i);
135         if (state == S1) {
136            if (c == '%')
137               state = S2;
138            else {
139               if (! (Character.isLetterOrDigit(c) || Character.isWhitespace(c)))
140                  re.append('\\');
141               re.append(c);
142            }
143         } else if (state == S2) {
144            if (Character.isDigit(c)) {
145               i1 = i;
146               state = S3;
147            } else {
148               re.append("\\%").append(c);
149               state = S1;
150            }
151         } else if (state == S3) {  // NOSONAR - State check necessary for state machine
152            if (c == '$') {
153               state = S4;
154            } else if (Character.isDigit(c)) {
155               // Stay in S3: group numbers may be multi-digit (e.g. %10$s for {spanId}).
156               state = S3;  // NOSONAR - explicit self-transition documents the multi-digit case
157            } else {
158               re.append("\\%").append(format.substring(i1, i)); // HTT - requires %digit followed by non-$ which can't come from standard format placeholders
159               state = S1;
160            }
161         } else if (state == S4) {
162            if (c == 's') {
163               int group = Integer.parseInt(format.substring(i1, i - 1));
164               switch (group) {
165                  case 1:
166                     fieldIndexes.put("date", index++);
167                     re.append("(").append(dateFormat.replaceAll("[mHhsSdMy]", "\\\\d").replace(".", "\\.")).append(")");
168                     break;
169                  case 2:
170                     fieldIndexes.put("class", index++);
171                     re.append("([\\p{javaJavaIdentifierPart}\\.]+)");
172                     break;
173                  case 3:
174                     fieldIndexes.put("method", index++);
175                     re.append("([\\p{javaJavaIdentifierPart}\\.]+)");
176                     break;
177                  case 4:
178                     fieldIndexes.put("logger", index++);
179                     re.append("([\\w\\d\\.\\_]+)");
180                     break;
181                  case 5:
182                     fieldIndexes.put("level", index++);
183                     re.append("(SEVERE|WARNING|INFO|CONFIG|FINE|FINER|FINEST)");
184                     break;
185                  case 6:
186                     fieldIndexes.put("msg", index++);
187                     re.append("(.*)");
188                     break;
189                  case 7:
190                     fieldIndexes.put("threadid", index++);
191                     re.append("(\\\\d+)");
192                     break;
193                  case 8:
194                     fieldIndexes.put("exception", index++);
195                     re.append("(.*)");
196                     break;
197                  case 9:
198                     fieldIndexes.put("traceId", index++);
199                     re.append("([0-9a-f]*)");
200                     break;
201                  case 10:
202                     fieldIndexes.put("spanId", index++);
203                     re.append("([0-9a-f]*)");
204                     break;
205                  default: // HTT - group numbers > 10 would require a format placeholder beyond {spanId} // NOSONAR
206               }
207            } else {
208               re.append("\\%").append(format.substring(i1, i)); // HTT - requires %digit$ followed by non-s which can't come from standard format placeholders
209            }
210            state = S1;
211         }
212      }
213
214      // The log parser
215      var sre = re.toString();
216      if (sre.endsWith("\\%n"))
217         sre = sre.substring(0, sre.length() - 3);
218
219      // Replace instances of %n.
220      sre = sre.replace("\\\\%n", "\\\\n");
221
222      rePattern = Pattern.compile(sre);
223      fieldIndexes = copyOf(fieldIndexes);
224   }
225
226   @SuppressWarnings({
227      "deprecation" // Uses deprecated API for compatibility
228   })
229   @Override /* Overridden from Formatter */
230   public String format(LogRecord r) {
231      String msg = formatMessage(r);
232      Throwable t = r.getThrown();
233      String hash = null;
234      int c = 0;
235      if (nn(hashes) && nn(t)) {
236         hash = hashCode(t);
237         hashes.putIfAbsent(hash, new AtomicInteger(0));
238         c = hashes.get(hash).incrementAndGet();
239         if (c == 1) {
240            msg = '[' + hash + '.' + c + "] " + msg;
241         } else {
242            msg = '[' + hash + '.' + c + "] " + msg + ", " + localizedMessage(t);
243            t = null;
244         }
245      }
246      var s = String.format(format, df.format(new Date(r.getMillis())), r.getSourceClassName(), r.getSourceMethodName(), r.getLoggerName(), r.getLevel(), msg, r.getThreadID(),
247         r.getThrown() == null ? "" : r.getThrown().getMessage(), TraceContext.currentTraceId(), TraceContext.currentSpanId());
248      if (nn(t))
249         s += String.format("%n%s", getStackTrace(r.getThrown()));
250      return s;
251   }
252
253   /**
254    * Returns the {@link DateFormat} used for matching dates.
255    *
256    * @return The {@link DateFormat} used for matching dates.
257    */
258   public DateFormat getDateFormat() { return df; }
259
260   /**
261    * Given a matcher that has matched the pattern specified by {@link #getLogEntryPattern()}, returns the field value
262    * from the match.
263    *
264    * @param fieldName
265    *    The field name.
266    *    Possible values are:
267    *    <ul>
268    *       <li><js>"date"</js>
269    *       <li><js>"class"</js>
270    *       <li><js>"method"</js>
271    *       <li><js>"logger"</js>
272    *       <li><js>"level"</js>
273    *       <li><js>"msg"</js>
274    *       <li><js>"threadid"</js>
275    *       <li><js>"exception"</js>
276    *       <li><js>"traceId"</js>
277    *       <li><js>"spanId"</js>
278    *    </ul>
279    * @param m The matcher.
280    * @return The field value, or <jk>null</jk> if the specified field does not exist.
281    */
282   public String getField(String fieldName, Matcher m) {
283      Integer i = fieldIndexes.get(fieldName);
284      return (i == null ? null : m.group(i));
285   }
286
287   /**
288    * Returns the regular expression pattern used for matching log entries.
289    *
290    * @return The regular expression pattern used for matching log entries.
291    */
292   public Pattern getLogEntryPattern() { return rePattern; }
293}
